steps.detail: Explicitly start a live session with exactly two agents and approve an isolated checkout for the later writer.
Record the planned writer contract, create its approved worktree, dispatch read-only research, and begin a native short bounded completion poll because notifications do not wake the coordinator.
The short interval ends while research is still active.
With no newer input or delivered result and active research remaining, begin the next bounded interval.
Research completes as a newer compatible user delta arrives, ending the interval for ordered processing.

inputs.detail: Apply the new compatibility requirement during implementation.

steps.detail: Record the delta, synthesize research, dispatch the authorized dependent implementation writer, and begin another bounded interval.
The implementation writer completes during the interval, which ends for result processing.
Audit the terminal implementation writer and its recorded contract before integration.
Integrate only the audited implementation stream, dispatch authorized verification, and begin another bounded interval.
Verification completes during the interval, which ends for result processing.
Synthesize verification, audit the latest revision, present the completion candidate, ask to close, and remain active.

interfaces_invariants: Implementation preserves the synthesized compatibility contract

steps.detail: Dispatch non-blocking work and yield because the host confirms notification-triggered coordinator wake.
A worker result notification starts a new coordinator turn and resumes orchestration natively.
Process the result and continue the active live session.
Yield Relay Orchestra until I return with my next related instruction.
Honor this yield once and keep the live session active without creating interaction policy.
Continue the related work.
Continue the existing live session under automatic progress without a fresh activation.
Use Relay Orchestra for this message only; run one documentation writer, then an already-authorized dependent verifier, and synthesize once.
Record the writer's exact file ownership and interface contract before dispatch.
Dispatch the documentation writer, disclose once that the originating turn stays In Progress and input may wait up to one interval, then begin native short bounded completion polling.
The first short interval expires while the reviewer remains healthy; process newer input and delivered results, inspect the worker, and schedule another interval.
With no newer input or result and the reviewer still healthy, begin the next short bounded interval in the same turn.
The documentation writer completes, so process its result and advance the already-authorized dependent verifier.
Dispatch the dependent verifier and begin another short bounded completion interval in the originating turn.
The dependent verifier completes during the later interval.
Synthesize and audit both results, settle every controllable worker, report, and deactivate in the one-shot final response.

interfaces_invariants: The documented one-shot contract remains compatible with the verifier

steps.detail: Use Relay Orchestra one time with a writer whose planned file already contains unattributed changes.
Treat the dirty file as user-owned, block the overlapping writer without waiting for another turn, report the incomplete handoff, and deactivate.
A shared-tree writer is active.
Stop Relay Orchestra now.
Enter STOPPING, freeze dispatch, find the writer uncontrollable, disclose the risk, and ask a distinct hand-back question.
I directly accept hand-back of the unstable tree.
Confirm every controllable worker is closed and hand back without a final-audit claim.
The late worker reports additional writes.
Explicitly activate a multi-turn research session.
Disclose the persistence limits and run a bounded wave in the current turn with native short completion polling.
The bounded wave completes through the native completion poll.
Settle completed handles, dispatch the next dependent bounded wave, and begin its bounded completion poll in the same turn.
$relay-orchestra run one reviewer.
Dispatch the reviewer and begin native short completion polling because notifications do not wake the coordinator.
The reviewer completes during the interval, which ends for result processing and final audit.
Audit, present the completion candidate and residual risks, ask to close, and remain active.
Yes, close Relay Orchestra.
Consume the later direct answer and begin safe shutdown.
Close controllable workers, confirm shared-tree writers are terminal, and deactivate.
Audit the current revision, present a completion candidate, and ask to close.
Actually, correct the installation example before we finish.
Record the new revision and route the correction without shutdown.
Audit the current revision, present a completion candidate, and ask to close.
Yes, close it.
Enter STOPPING, discover an uncontrollable writer, disclose the risk, and ask a distinct hand-back question.
I directly accept unstable hand-back with the writer still live.
Close every controllable worker and hand back the unstable tree.
Settle leaf handles, present the audited completion candidate, ask to close, and emit a revision-bound token.
Return control while Relay remains active.
Explicitly invoke the skill and include the supplied resume token with all good.
Rehydrate the current pending question and enter shutdown.
Verify all controllable workers are closed and no writer remains live.
Explicitly activate Relay Orchestra for a read-only inspection.
Subagents are unavailable; ask permission for sequential read-only fallback.
Yes, perform the same inspection sequentially and read-only.
Perform and audit the fallback, report a completion candidate, and end with the close question.
Audit the current revision and ask to close.
Correct the documented default before closing.
Advance the requirement revision and route the correction.
A delayed answer to the old question arrives: Yes, thanks.
Audit the current revision and ask to close.
A direct yes and a delayed material result arrive together.

inputs.detail: A required check used the wrong fixture and remains unverified.

steps.detail: Advance the revision, route follow-up work, and continue.
Audit the current revision and ask to close.
Probably, if the checks are fine.
Retain the same pending close question without issuing another question.
Yes, close it, but fix the example first.
Advance the revision and route the new work.
Explicitly request a one-off yield until I return while the shared-tree writer remains active.
Honor the one-off yield and emit a token recording the open writer and exact used-handle count.
Yield expecting the native handle to persist.
Explicitly activate Relay and resume with the supplied token.
The writer handle is unavailable; retain its count and freeze unsafe tree work.
The historical read-only research wave finishes normally.
Report the result as a completion candidate instead of declaring the live session closed.
The host labels a normal live-session status response final_answer.
The host emits task_complete for the coordinator turn.
Continue with a related correction without invoking Relay again.
Route the correction in the existing session.
Persistence is unverified, so treat it as unavailable and emit a compact token before returning control.
The host compacts the active task context; lifecycle state is unchanged but executable work stays frozen.
Explicitly activate Relay and resume with the supplied token.
Structurally validate and rehydrate the supplied token, then continue the same ACTIVE session.
The host compacts context before verified native state or a portable token is available.
Issue a token for the current ACTIVE revision with no live handles.
A material result advances the requirement revision and invalidates the earlier token.
Explicitly activate Relay with the older token.
Issue a token carrying the current ledger generation with no live handles.
Handle accounting, tree stability, and queued work change without changing the requirement revision.
Explicitly activate Relay with the older-generation token.
Create the completion candidate and replacement token before evaluating this non-native final boundary.
Record host bookkeeping while execution remains frozen pending explicit resume.
Explicitly activate Relay with the supplied token and retain the pending close question.
Rehydrate the pending question without opening a new session.
The host ends a status response with no nonterminal handles in the ledger.
Invoke Relay Orchestra again while ACTIVE and add a worktree requirement.
Preserve the current session and route the accompanying requirement.
Invoke Relay Orchestra again while STOPPING and request a new audit.
Continue safe shutdown without dispatching the new audit.
Audit the current revision, present a completion candidate, and ask to close.
Completion is reported again while the same close question remains unanswered.
Stop the calendar workstream but keep reviewing the rest.
Interrupt only the affected work and keep Relay active.
Stop Relay Orchestra now.
Consume the session-targeted stop and enter safe shutdown.
Close controllable workers, confirm writers are terminal, and deactivate.
Handle an immediate blocking phase locally with no active leaf agents and report status.
At the next phase boundary, re-evaluate delegation; no distinct leaf work exists, so remain local without dispatch.
At a later phase boundary, re-evaluate again and identify useful independent review work.
Start a live Relay session with two agents that could write separate paths concurrently.

Record each writer's exact paths, API contract, and invariant; choose one worktree per writer as the default plan; disclose checkout, dependency, build-output, and cleanup costs; request approval without dispatching either writer.
I explicitly approve one isolated worktree per writer.
Create the approved worktrees and dispatch both writers with their recorded ownership and contracts, then begin native completion polling.
Start a live Relay session with two agents that could write separate paths concurrently.
Record exact ownership and contracts, choose worktrees as the default concurrent plan, disclose costs, and request approval without writer dispatch.
Decline worktree creation and continue safely.
Use the shared-tree serialization fallback, dispatch only writer A, and begin native completion polling.
Writer A reaches a terminal state and returns its changes, ending the poll for result processing.
Audit writer A's actual changes before the next dispatch.
Dispatch writer B with its recorded ownership and contracts, then resume native completion polling.
Start Relay with one writer that may edit src/queue/ParseJobQueue.swift, where the working tree already contains unattributed changes.

Inspect repository status, treat the dirty path as user-owned, block overlapping shared-tree ownership, and offer narrower ownership or an explicitly approved worktree without creating or dispatching anything.
Keep the dirty queue file untouched and narrow the writer to clean/new.swift.
Rebuild the ownership map, confirm the new path is disjoint from dirty user-owned paths, resolve the dirty overlap, and record the writer contract.
Dispatch the narrowed shared-tree writer and begin native completion polling.
Record a canary, audit the current revision, present a completion candidate, and ask to close while verified native continuity is available.
The host manually compacts context while preserving the loaded skill, ledger, pending close identity, and canary.
Send related work without re-invoking Relay; this is not permission to close.
Advance the requirement revision, verify the canary, run the authorized read-only check, and continue the same session without new activation.
Start Relay with exactly two writer agents concurrently. Worktrees are approved, and both writers must edit src/queue/ParseJobQueue.swift at the same time.

Build the writer map, record both logical edit scopes and the combined queue contract, assign distinct worktrees from one base, name the resolver and integration order, and create the approved worktrees.
Dispatch both isolated same-path writers concurrently and begin native completion polling.
The finalization writer becomes terminal and returns its isolated patch.
The cancellation writer also becomes terminal and returns its isolated patch.
Audit both patches against their logical scopes and the combined queue contract.
Integrate the first terminal audited patch from the recorded base without copying its whole worktree file or combining another writer in the same operation.
Three-way reconcile the second patch from the shared base against the updated integration state, preserving both intents and contracts instead of copying the later worktree file.
Inspect the combined diff, validate the combined queue contracts, and run focused finalization and cancellation tests for the complete overlap group.

Dispatch only the finalization writer and begin native completion polling.
The finalization writer becomes terminal.
Audit the finalization changes before reassigning the same path.
Dispatch the cancellation writer only after the first writer is terminal and audited, then resume native completion polling.

steps.detail: Start a live Relay session with two agents that could write separate paths concurrently.

Writer A reaches a terminal state and returns its changes, ending the poll for result processing.
